case_title: TEAMSTERS LOCAL UNION NO. 856 VS. SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, NTC OF MTN FOR PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE CONTINUED FROM MAR-06-2014 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAR-19-2014, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER PARTIES AGREEMENT. (D302)
REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL UNION NO. 856

LAW AND MOTION, 302, COURT DISCLOSURE: IN THE EARLY 1970'S, FOR ABOUT 3 YEARS, MY MOTHER WAS A MEMBER OF THE TEAMSTERS UNION. I DON'T KNOW THE LOCAL. IT WAS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. DURING THAT TIME I WAS AWARDED A COLLEGE SCHOLARSHIP FROM THE TEAMSTERS UNION, FOR $6,000.00. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL HEARING THIS CASE. I BELIEVE A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD NOT REASONABLY ENTERTAIN A DOUBT THAT I WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. RULING: PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL UNION NO. 856'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976 (302)

Ntc Of Mtn For Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sarah Sandford-Smith, Esq., 650.635.0111, for Petitioner Teamsters Local Union No. 856.
Randy Riddle, Esq. and Ivan Delventhal, Esq., 415.678.38200, for Respondent San Francisco Zoological Society.

COURT DISCLOSURE: IN THE EARLY 1970'S, FOR ABOUT 3 YEARS, MY MOTHER WAS A MEMBER OF THE TEAMSTERS UNION. I DON'T KNOW THE LOCAL. IT WAS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. DURING THAT TIME I WAS AWARDED A COLLEGE SCHOLARSHIP FROM THE TEAMSTERS UNION, FOR $6,000.00. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL HEARING THIS CASE. I BELIEVE A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD NOT REASONABLY ENTERTAIN A DOUBT THAT I WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL.

RULING: The Court issues its tentative ruling in open court: PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL UNION NO. 856'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate is denied. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling denying the petition for writ of mandate. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
